message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate the 1B characterization matrix. Write /tmp/onebe_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/onebe_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -25' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
Plan V4 1B: lock today's config behaviour across {unset,empty,valid,malformed}x{default/.env/process-env/CLI precedence}x{key-case}x{read-timing lazy vs snapshot}x{runtime type str/coerced/SecretStr} — the real 'byte-identical' proof. Evidence: design={"type_reps":[{"type":"a_plain_str","keys":["ANTIWORD_CMD","CHROMA_PERSIST_DIR"],"registry_default":{"ANTIWORD_CMD":"antiword","CHROMA_PERSIST_DIR":"data/chroma_db"},"read_via":"resolve(); returned as raw str, no coercion","notes":"Pure str pass-through of os.getenv(key, default). Byte-identical characterization: unset->default, set->override."},{"type":"b_int_coerced","keys":["PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR","PG_MAX_SECTIONS"],"registry_default":{"PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR":"50","PG_MAX_SECTIONS":"15"},"coercion_site":"int(resolve('PG_...')) in src/polaris_graph/state.py:23 and :44","notes":"Registry stores str; call site coerces with int(). Malformed value -> ValueError raised at call site (module import time). Empty-string value would also raise."},{"type":"c_float_coerced","keys":["PG_MIN_FAITHFULNESS","PG_CROSS_REF_SIM_THRESHOLD"],"registry_default":{"PG_MIN_FAITHFULNESS":"0.70","PG_CROSS_REF_SIM_THRESHOLD":"0.65"},"coercion_site":"float(resolve(...)) in src/polaris_graph/state.py:33 and src/polaris_graph/agents/cross_reference.py:47","notes":"Threshold keys. float() coercion at call site; malformed -> ValueError."},{"type":"d_bool_key","keys":["PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED","PG_AMPLIFICATION_ENABLED"],"registry_default":{"PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED":"0","PG_AMPLIFICATION_ENABLED":"1"},"coercion_site":"resolve('PG_...') == '1' in src/polaris_graph/checkpoint_manager.py:26 and src/polaris_graph/state.py:80","notes":"Boolean via =="1" comparison at call site. NEVER raises: any non-'1' value (malformed, empty, unset->'0') yields False. Only literal '1' is True."},{"type":"e_model_key","keys":["PG_JUDGE_MODEL","PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL"],"registry_default":{"PG_JUDGE_MODEL":"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b","PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL":null},"read_via":"ModelSettings field via get_model_settings() (pydantic, validation_alias, case_sensitive=True); PG_JUDGE_MODEL is str non-empty default, PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL is 'str | None' with None default","notes":"Distinct from resolve() layer. 12 model keys total. Case-sensitive: lowercase alias (pg_judge_model) is IGNORED. Fresh instance each call = live read. PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL also present in CONFIG_DEFAULTS with None default (dual-registered)."},{"type":"f_secret_shaped","keys":["OPENROUTER_API_KEY","EXA_API_KEY","ZYTE_API_KEY"],"registry_default":{"OPENROUTER_API_KEY":"","EXA_API_KEY":"","ZYTE_API_KEY":null},"read_via":"resolve() as plain str/None -- NOT SecretStr","notes":"KEY/TOKEN/SECRET-shaped keys are plain strings, no masking. OPENROUTER_API_KEY & EXA_API_KEY have '' default; ZYTE_API_KEY has None default (returns None when unset)."},{"type":"g_empty_string_default","keys":["PG_PLANNING_GATE_MODEL","PG_POLICY_MODEL","OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER"],"registry_default":{"PG_PLANNING_GATE_MODEL":"","PG_POLICY_MODEL":"","OPENROUTER_PROVIDER_ORDER":""},"notes":"Empty-string default: resolve() returns '' when unset (falsy sentinel meaning 'auto/disabled'). PG_PLANNING_GATE_MODEL & PG_POLICY_MODEL ALSO exist as ModelSettings fields with '' default (dual-registered str)."},{"type":"h_unset_in_registry","keys":["<any key not in CONFIG_DEFAULTS, e.g. PG_TOTALLY_UNREGISTERED_KEY_XYZ>"],"registry_default":"N/A","notes":"resolve() raises KeyError for a key not present in CONFIG_DEFAULTS (intentional: multi-line/computed/conflicting defaults excluded, migrated manually). Verified empirically."}],"axes_applicable":["value-state: unset (returns registry default)","value-state: empty-string (returns '' for str keys; ValueError for int/float-coerced call sites; False for bool =="1")","value-state: valid (override read live from env)","value-state: malformed (str: passes through raw; int/float: ValueError at call site; bool: silently False)","precedence: registry-default vs process-env override (os.getenv live read; process env wins)","precedence: .env layer EXISTS via load_dotenv() default override=False -- process env takes precedence over .env, .env over registry default","precedence: NO dedicated CLI layer in config module (a few scripts set os.environ at startup = plain process-env, not a resolve()/ModelSettings precedence tier)","key-case-sensitivity: ModelSettings uses case_sensitive=True (lowercase alias ignored); resolve()/os.getenv is inherently case-sensitive","read-timing: resolve() reads live env EVERY call; ModelSettings SNAPSHOTS env at construction, but get_model_settings() returns a FRESH instance per call => effectively live","runtime-type: str (resolve plain) vs coerced-at-callsite (int/float/bool) vs pydantic-typed (ModelSettings str / str|None). NO SecretStr type in this codebase"],"coercion_sites_exist":true,"secretstr_exists":false,"baseline":"16738 tests collected, 11 collection errors (via /home/polaris/conda_cu128/bin/python -m pytest --collect-only at /home/polaris/wt/phase-1b on branch chore/review-readiness-1b). Errors are pre-existing collection failures (playwright TargetClosedError, mesh/benchmark import errors) unrelated to config. Worktree created, oracle overlaid by copy (tests/oracle/*.py + cassettes/*). 871 keys in CONFIG_DEFAULTS, 12 model keys in MODEL_KEY_DEFAULTS. No SecretStr anywhere in src/. Coercion (int/float/==\"1\") happens at call sites, not in the config layer.""}, write={"tests_added":118,"all_pass":true,"matrix_cells_covered":118,"notes":"Wrote /home/polaris/wt/phase-1b/tests/test_config_characterization_matrix.py. 118 tests, all PASS (0.15s), hermetic (monkeypatch env only, no network/fs). Command: /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_config_characterization_matrix.py -q | tail -3 -> "118 passed".

Coverage across all 8 representative axes x value-state x precedence:
- Core identity: every representative key asserts resolve(key) == os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key]) byte-for-byte across {unset->registry default, valid override via monkeypatch.setenv, empty '', malformed}. Malformed/empty pass through raw at the resolve() layer (resolve NEVER raises on a value).
- Axis A (plain str): no coercion, no stripping; type is exactly str.
- Axis B (int, state.py:23/:44): int(resolve(k)) parses unset default + valid; malformed AND empty '' raise ValueError at the CALL SITE (documented resolve() itself does not raise).
- Axis C (float, state.py:33 / cross_reference.py:47): same pattern, float() raises on malformed + '' at call site.
- Axis D (bool ==\"1\", checkpoint_manager.py:26 / state.py:80): NEVER raises; only literal '1' is True; parametrized 10 non-'1' values (incl. '', 'true', ' 1', '01', malformed) all False; verified opposite defaults (CHECKPOINT '0'->False, AMPLIFICATION '1'->True).
- Axis E (model keys, ModelSettings pydantic): get_model_settings().field for unset default (judge='qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b', evaluator=None) + valid override; case_sensitive=True asserted (lowercase pg_judge_model IGNORED, correct-case wins); PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL dual-registration (CONFIG_DEFAULTS None + ModelSettings None) agrees across both layers.
- Axis F (secret-shaped): asserts type(val) is str (NOT SecretStr) and value exposed verbatim, no masking -- documents the pre-SecretStr baseline so a future SecretStr pass must consciously update it. Defaults: OPENROUTER/EXA '' , ZYTE None.
- Axis G (empty-string default): '' sentinel when unset.
- Axis H (unregistered key): resolve() raises KeyError even when the env var IS set (registry is the gate, not env).
- Read-timing/freshness: resolve() reads live env on every call (set-after-import seen); get_model_settings() returns a fresh non-cached instance per call, old snapshot not mutated.
- Precedence: process-env strictly beats registry default for every representative; documented there is NO dedicated CLI tier inside resolve()/ModelSettings.

All assertions LOCK current behaviour (characterization, not specification)."}, verify={"new_tests_pass":true,"collection_delta_ok":true,"oracle_matches":false,"oracle_sha":""}.
Ask codex: "Do these characterization tests genuinely cover the 1B axes that APPLY to this codebase (value-state, precedence, case, timing, coercion/type, SecretStr-or-its-absence), for a representative key per type? Are they true characterization (asserting current behaviour) rather than aspirational? Any axis that applies but is untested? End with 1B-OK or 1B-REVISE (+ the missing axis)."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate file and run CODEX to evaluate the 1B characterization matrix.

input.content:
You are gating a "characterization matrix" test suite for Plan V4 Phase 1B of a code-review-readiness initiative. The goal of 1B is to LOCK today's config-reading behaviour byte-identically across every axis that actually applies to THIS codebase, so a future refactor (e.g. introducing SecretStr, or centralizing coercion) provably preserves behaviour. These are CHARACTERIZATION tests (assert current behaviour, even if ugly), not aspirational/spec tests.

## The axes (as claimed applicable to this codebase)
value-state {unset, empty, valid, malformed} x precedence {registry-default, .env, process-env, CLI} x key-case x read-timing {lazy/live vs snapshot} x runtime-type {plain str, coerced int/float/bool at call site, pydantic ModelSettings str|None} x SecretStr-or-its-absence.

## DESIGN evidence (what the codebase actually does)
- Two config layers: (1) `resolve(key)` = `os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key])` — 871 keys, plain live read, NEVER raises on a value, raises KeyError if key absent from CONFIG_DEFAULTS. (2) `ModelSettings` pydantic (12 model keys) via `get_model_settings()`, case_sensitive=True, validation_alias, fresh instance per call.
- Coercion happens AT CALL SITES, not in config layer:
  - int: `int(resolve('PG_QUERIES_PER_VECTOR'))` state.py:23, PG_MAX_SECTIONS :44 — malformed AND empty '' raise ValueError at call site (import time).
  - float: `float(resolve(...))` state.py:33, cross_reference.py:47 (PG_MIN_FAITHFULNESS, PG_CROSS_REF_SIM_THRESHOLD) — same ValueError pattern.
  - bool: `resolve('PG_...') == '1'` checkpoint_manager.py:26, state.py:80 — NEVER raises; only literal '1' is True; every other value (unset '0', '', 'true', malformed) is False.
- Representative types: (a) plain str ANTIWORD_CMD/CHROMA_PERSIST_DIR; (b) int-coerced; (c) float-coerced; (d) bool-key; (e) ModelSettings PG_JUDGE_MODEL (str default) / PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL (str|None, None default, DUAL-registered in CONFIG_DEFAULTS too); (f) secret-shaped OPENROUTER_API_KEY/EXA_API_KEY ('' default)/ZYTE_API_KEY (None default) — plain str, NO SecretStr, no masking; (g) empty-string-default sentinel keys; (h) unregistered key -> KeyError.
- Precedence facts: `load_dotenv()` with default override=False, so process-env > .env > registry default. NO dedicated CLI precedence tier inside resolve()/ModelSettings (a few scripts set os.environ at startup = plain process-env, not a distinct tier). SecretStr does NOT exist anywhere in src/.
- read-timing: resolve() reads live env EVERY call; ModelSettings snapshots at construction but get_model_settings() returns a fresh instance per call => effectively live.

## WRITE evidence (what the tests do)
118 tests in tests/test_config_characterization_matrix.py, all PASS (0.15s), hermetic (monkeypatch env only, no net/fs). Coverage:
- Core identity: every representative key asserts resolve(key) == os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key]) byte-for-byte across {unset->default, valid override, empty '', malformed}. resolve() never raises on a value.
- Axis A str: no coercion/stripping, type exactly str.
- Axis B int: int(resolve) parses unset default + valid; malformed AND '' raise ValueError at call site (documented resolve itself doesn't raise).
- Axis C float: same, float() raises on malformed + '' at call site.
- Axis D bool ==\"1\": never raises; only '1' True; parametrized 10 non-'1' values (incl '', 'true', ' 1', '01') all False; opposite defaults verified (CHECKPOINT '0'->False, AMPLIFICATION '1'->True).
- Axis E model keys: get_model_settings().field for unset default (judge, evaluator=None) + valid override; case_sensitive=True asserted (lowercase pg_judge_model IGNORED); PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL dual-registration agrees across both layers.
- Axis F secret-shaped: asserts type(val) is str (NOT SecretStr), value verbatim/no masking — documents pre-SecretStr baseline so a future SecretStr pass must consciously update it. Defaults OPENROUTER/EXA '', ZYTE None.
- Axis G empty-string default: '' sentinel when unset.
- Axis H unregistered: resolve() raises KeyError even when env IS set (registry is the gate, not env).
- Read-timing/freshness: resolve() live env on every call (set-after-import seen); get_model_settings() fresh non-cached instance per call, old snapshot not mutated.
- Precedence: process-env strictly beats registry default for every representative; documented NO dedicated CLI tier.

## VERIFY evidence
new_tests_pass=true, collection_delta_ok=true (16738 collected, 11 pre-existing unrelated collection errors), oracle_matches=false/oracle_sha="" (deterministic oracle not matched — note this).

## Your question
Do these characterization tests genuinely cover the 1B axes that APPLY to this codebase (value-state, precedence, case, timing, coercion/type, SecretStr-or-its-absence), for a representative key per type? Are they true characterization (asserting current behaviour) rather than aspirational? Is there any axis that APPLIES but is untested? Consider specifically: (1) the .env precedence layer — is asserting only process-env>registry-default sufficient, or must .env>registry and process-env>.env be independently locked? (2) does ModelSettings need value-state {empty, malformed} coverage or is str|None enough? (3) is the oracle_matches=false a blocker?

Be concise and rigorous. End your answer with exactly one line: either "1B-OK" or "1B-REVISE (<the missing axis/axes>)".
